01_initial_request.input_data: Grosu's (1973) decomposition of Ross's (1967) Coordinate Structure Constraint and Chomsky's (1991) Least Effort Principle
01_initial_request.user_intent: To search for a principled account for the Conjunct Constraint and defend the proposal linking its effects to specific factors
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Deduction of the Coordinand Constraint from the Least Effort Principle

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The Coordinate Structure Constraint (CSC) is a widely accepted syntactic principle. [Gap] It is unclear if CSC is a unified principle or decomposable. [Decision] To achieve a principled account, chose to use Grosu's Decomposition Method to perform separation of CSC into Coordinand Constraint and Element Constraint on Ross's (1967) formulation, expecting to obtain independent principles for investigation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: CSC is decomposed into two principles: CC (No coordinand may be moved) and EC (No element may be moved out of a coordinand).

references.relevance: Provides the decomposition of CSC into CC and EC.
Original formulation of the Coordinate Structure Constraint.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Coordination requires a formal definition to analyze constraints. [Gap] Standard definitions do not formally encode category selection. [Decision] To achieve formal precision, chose to use Categorial Grammar Formalism to perform definition of the coordinator head on syntactic categories, expecting to obtain a categorematic definition of Coord.

parameters.requirement: Coordinands and resulting coordinate structure must be of category X

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Coordinator is defined as a function selecting two arguments of category X and yielding a coordinate phrase of category X.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Categorial identity is needed to determine valid coordinands. [Gap] Traditional binary features [N]/[V] are insufficient. [Decision] To achieve accurate distributional capture, chose to use Feature-based Definition of Categorial Identity to perform specification of categorial and bar-level features on syntactic constituents, expecting to obtain the Coordinability Condition.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Two constituents can be coordinated if and only if they share the same categorial and bar-level features.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single coordinand extraction is ungrammatical. [Gap] The syntactic reason for this restriction needs explanation. [Decision] To achieve explanation of Case 1, chose to use Probe-Goal Relations (Minimalist Program) to perform analysis of feature mismatch on extracted vs. remaining coordinands, expecting to obtain evidence of categorial distinctness.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Extraction of a single coordinand results in coordination of categorially distinct constituents, violating the Coordinability Condition.

references.relevance: Framework for internal Merge and Agree operations.
Source for cartographic approach and criterial features.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Extraction of both categorially identical coordinands is also ungrammatical. [Gap] Standard locality conditions do not fully explain this. [Decision] To achieve explanation of Case 2, chose to use Integrity Condition on Coordinate Structures to perform restriction of probe targeting on coordinate phrases, expecting to obtain preeminence of the coordinate structure over coordinands.

parameters.condition: Probe H targets coordinate structure X, not [+F] category contained within X
parameters.economy_basis: Avoids redundant derivations and restricts visible goals

02_agent_trajectory.observation: The coordinate structure is identified as the sole possible goal for a probe, prohibiting extraction of individual coordinands.

references.relevance: Discussion of multiple wh-movement out of coordination.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Across-the-board dependencies sometimes imply self-coordination. [Gap] Self-coordination is semantically vacuous. [Decision] To achieve explanation of Pattern 3, chose to use Prohibition against Self-Coordination to perform filtering of vacuous syntactic operations on constituent merging, expecting to obtain ban on coordinating a constituent to itself.

parameters.constraint: A syntactic constituent cannot be coordinated to itself

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Constructions involving coordination of a constituent to itself are ungrammatical due to vacuity.

references.relevance: Source for Prohibition against Vacuous Quantification analogy.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Three factors explain CC effects but need a unified principle. [Gap] It is unclear if these factors are primitive. [Decision] To achieve quantifiable goal, chose to use Least Effort Principle to perform deduction of the three factors on grammatical derivations, expecting to obtain derivation of CC from general economy principles.

parameters.principle: Grammatical derivations contain no vacuous or superfluous steps

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All three factors are deduced from the requirement to avoid vacuous or superfluous steps in derivation.

references.relevance: Original formulation of the Least Effort Principle.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Theoretical Deduction and Grammaticality Judgment Analysis

constraint_deduction.interpretation: The Coordinand Constraint is successfully deduced from the Least Effort Principle via three intermediate factors.

factor_decomposition.interpretation: Three distinct factors (lexical, economy, interface) identified and linked to the principle.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective to deduce the Coordinand Constraint from the Least Effort Principle was achieved by decomposing the constraint into three factors derived from economy and interface conditions.
